title: 顺水推舟
summary:
Hercule Poirot enters an empty hall at an inn and observes the quiet atmosphere of the guest area. He visits room number five, which appears recently cleaned and polished, lacking its usual Axminster rug. Upon examining the room's furnishings, including a marble fireplace, Poirot wipes his finger along the right side of the fireplace surround and finds it blackened, while the left side remains clean. He also notes a window overlooking a garage roof and a narrow alley that provides a discreet shortcut.

Seeking warmth, Poirot returns to the "guest-only" room, where he encounters a formidable, elderly woman with iron-gray hair. The woman expresses xenophobic views, criticizing foreigners, the quality of the inn's food, and the changing social morals of young women. She complains about the presence of soldiers and pilots, as well as the lack of proper modesty in women's clothing.

The conversation shifts when the woman reveals she witnessed a woman in an orange scarf and makeup exiting room number five late at night, after ten o'clock. The woman claims she saw this individual laughing and chatting with a man in the room. This event occurred the night before the man was murdered. The old lady expresses her disapproval of the woman's behavior and mentions she has already complained to Miss Lippincott about such indiscretions.
